title: Stock vs Flow

content.statement: Everything is what you have (stock) or what changes it (flow).
content.question: Are you optimizing the stock or the flow? Which actually matters?
content.business_lens: Followers = stock. Content = inflow. Unfollows = outflow.
sop: Name your key stock: followers, cash, inventory, or client roster.
Identify the Inflow and Outflow that change it.
Track Inflow and Outflow weekly, not just the stock total (see calculation, page 6).
Focus your effort on improving the flow rate, not obsessing over the current stock number.
Recalculate the stock monthly to confirm the flows are actually moving it in the right direction.
questions: Are you optimizing the stock or the flow? Which actually matters?
What's your current stock number, and could you actually name the inflow and outflow that are changing it right now?
If your stock stayed flat this month, would you know whether that's because nothing moved, or because inflow and outflow just cancelled out?

definitions.def: A "stock" is what you have; a "flow" is what changes it.

content.equation_example: You had 4,200 followers last month. This month you gained 350 new followers, Inflow, and lost 90, Outflow. New stock equals 4,200 plus 350 minus 90 = 4,460 followers. A rising stock means inflow is outpacing outflow, the underlying flows are healthy even if the stock number moves slowly. A flat or falling stock means outflow is quietly cancelling out your gains, even while you're technically 'growing.'
content.closing_statement: Watch the flows; the stock takes care of itself.

caption:
Principle Nº 117: Stock vs Flow

Everything is what you have (stock) or what changes it (flow).

Ask yourself: Are you optimizing the stock or the flow? Which actually matters?
